question.text: Under the FDI route of inbound investment, the minimum investment threshold to qualify as FDI is:

answer.text: 5% of post-investment fully diluted paid-up capital
10% of post-investment fully diluted paid-up capital
15% of post-investment fully diluted paid-up capital
25% of post-investment fully diluted paid-up capital

explanation.text: **The Logic:** FDI is defined as a strategic/long-term investment of 10% or more of post-investment fully diluted paid-up capital. Investment below 10% in a listed company would typically fall under the FPI route. **The Trap:** "25%" is the SAST trigger threshold for open offer obligations, not the FDI classification threshold. Candidates often confuse these two distinct 10% and 25% thresholds. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series XII (Securities Market Foundation) tests FDI vs. FPI classification in detail. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 10% line is the critical divider — below it you are a portfolio investor (FPI), above it you are a strategic investor (FDI) with different regulatory obligations.
